info.title: Formo Public API
info.description:
REST API for managing Formo projects, analytics, alerts, boards, charts, contracts, segments, and AI chat.

**Auth.** All endpoints require a workspace API key with the appropriate scopes — see `x-api-scopes`.

**Response shape.** Successful responses return the resource directly (or `{ data: [...], total, page, size, has_more }` for paginated lists). HTTP status carries success/failure — there is no envelope wrapping success bodies.

**Errors.** Every non-2xx response uses the `Error` envelope: `{ error: { code, message, doc_url, param?, details? } }`. Branch on the machine-readable `code` (see `ErrorCode` enum) and follow `doc_url` to the matching section of the [errors reference](https://docs.formo.so/api/errors).

**Idempotency.** Pass an `Idempotency-Key` header on POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE to make retries safe; the response is cached for 24 h and replayed on duplicate keys.

servers.description: API Server (boards, alerts, contracts, segments, profiles, query, import)

WorkspaceApiKey.description: Workspace API key (e.g. `formo_xxx`). Create one in the Formo dashboard under Team Settings > API Keys.

Error.description: Standard error envelope returned by every public API endpoint for any non-2xx response. The HTTP status code carries success/failure; the body provides a machine-readable `code`, a human-readable `message`, and a `doc_url` pointing at the matching section of the docs so agents can fetch context on the fly.

message.description: Human-readable error description. Wording may change between releases — branch on `code`, not `message`.

doc_url.description: Link to the matching section of the errors reference at https://docs.formo.so/api/errors.

param.description: When the error pertains to a specific request field, the dotted path to that field (e.g. `body.trigger_filters.0.value`).

details.description: Code-specific extra context. For `INVALID_VALIDATION_REQUEST` this is a `{ fieldPath: message }` map of every Zod validation failure.

ErrorCode.description: Stable, enumerated error codes. New codes may be added in any release; clients should treat unknown codes as the closest matching HTTP status family.

enabled.description: Whether the board is publicly accessible

StepFilterCondition.description: A filter condition for an event property on a funnel step. The `op` field specifies the comparison operator and `value` is the value to compare against. For `in` and `notIn` operators the value is a pipe-delimited string (e.g. `"metamask|rainbow|coinbase"`).

op.description: Comparison operator. Use `in` / `notIn` for multi-value matching (pipe-delimited value string).

value.description: The comparison value. For `in` / `notIn`, use a pipe-delimited string: `"metamask|rainbow|coinbase"`.

FunnelStep.description:
A single step in a funnel or user-path flow.

`type` and `event` are required. Any additional property key becomes a filter condition using the `StepFilterCondition` schema (e.g. `"rdns": { "op": "equals", "value": "io.metamask" }`).

Standard filterable columns: `origin`, `device`, `browser`, `os`, `location`, `referrer`, `ref`, `utm_source`, `utm_medium`, `utm_campaign`, `utm_content`, `utm_term`. Any other key is treated as a JSON event property and extracted via `JSONExtractString(properties, '<key>')`.

type.description: `event` — built-in page/connect/transaction events; `track` — custom tracked events; `decoded_log` — decoded smart-contract events.

event.description: Event name (e.g. `page`, `connect`, `transaction`, or a custom track event name).

ConversionWindow.description: Time window within which a user must complete all funnel steps (measured from Step 1). Defaults to 1 day if omitted.

AnalyticsFilterCondition.description: A single analytics filter condition. Use `in` / `notIn` with a pipe-delimited string value (e.g. `"a|b|c"`) for multi-value matches.

field.description: Column to filter on. Standard session columns: `device`, `browser`, `os`, `location`, `referrer`, `ref`, `origin`, `utm_source`, `utm_medium`, `utm_campaign`, `utm_content`, `utm_term`. Track-event columns: `event`, `type`. Numeric event properties: `volume`, `revenue`, `points`.

value.description: Value to compare against. For `in` / `notIn`, pass a pipe-delimited string (e.g. `"chrome|firefox"`).

RetentionUserFilter.description: A user-level segment filter applied to the retention chart cohort.

field.description: The user property to filter on (e.g. `device`, `browser`, `os`, `location`, `utm_source`, `utm_medium`, `utm_campaign`).

ChartSettings.description:
Chart-type-specific configuration. The fields that apply depend on `chart_type`:

- **funnel**: `funnelType`, `conversionWindow`, `breakdown`
- **user_paths**: `startStep`, `endStep`, `maxSteps`, `nodesPerStep`, `conversionWindow`, `filters`
- **retention**: `retentionFilter`, `retentionUserFilters`

All fields are optional at the schema level; see per-type validation rules for which are functionally required.

funnelType.description: **Funnel only.** `closed` — users must complete steps in strict order with no intervening events. `open` — users may complete steps in order but other events may occur between steps.

conversionWindow.description: **Funnel & user_paths.** Maximum time from Step 1 for a user to complete all steps.

breakdown.description: **Funnel only.** Split each funnel bar by this dimension. The top categories are shown individually; the rest are collapsed into 'Others'.

startStep.description: **user_paths — required.** The event where the user flow begins.

endStep.description: **user_paths — optional.** The event where the user flow ends. If `null` the flow is open-ended.

maxSteps.description: **user_paths.** Maximum number of steps to show in the flow (2–10). Values above 10 are clamped to 10.

nodesPerStep.description: **user_paths.** Maximum number of unique event nodes visible per step (2–10). Values above 10 are clamped to 10.

filters.description: **user_paths.** JSON-encoded string of additional filters applied to the path query.

retentionFilter.description: **retention.** Event that qualifies a returning visit as 'retained'. If `null`, any event counts as a return.

retentionUserFilters.description: **retention.** Zero or more user-segment filters that narrow the cohort (e.g. only desktop users, only users from a specific UTM source).

CreateChartRequest.description: Request body for creating a chart.

query.description:
SQL query that powers the chart.

- **`funnel`** — pass `"SELECT 1"`; the actual query is auto-generated from `steps`.
- **`retention`** — can be omitted or pass `""`; data is fetched from the retention pipe directly.
- **All other types** — required; must be a valid SQL string.

chart_type.description:
Visualization type. Determines which other fields are required:

| `chart_type` | Extra required fields |
|---|---|
| `table` | `query` |
| `number` | `query` (must return 1 row × 1 column) |
| `bar` | `query`, `x_axis`, `y_axis` (≥ 1) |
| `line` | `query`, `x_axis`, `y_axis` (≥ 1) |
| `pie` | `query`, `y_axis` (exactly 1) |
| `stacked` | `query`, `x_axis`, `y_axis` (exactly 1), `group_by` |
| `funnel` | `steps` (≥ 2), `query` placeholder `"SELECT 1"` |
| `user_paths` | `query`, `settings.startStep` |
| `retention` | none (`query` ignored) |

title.description: Display name shown on the chart and board.

x_axis.description: Column name for the X axis. Required for `bar`, `line`, and `stacked`.

y_axis.description:
Column name(s) used as Y axis metrics.

- `bar` / `line` — at least 1 element required.
- `pie` / `stacked` — exactly 1 element required.

group_by.description: Column to group / stack series by. Required for `stacked`.

steps.description:
Ordered list of funnel steps. Required for `funnel` (minimum 2 steps).

Each element is a `FunnelStep` — add property filters as extra keys on the step object (e.g. `"rdns": { "op": "equals", "value": "io.metamask" }`).

UpdateChartRequest.description: Request body for updating an existing chart.

chartId.description: ID of the chart to update.

query.description:
SQL query that powers the chart.

- **`funnel`** — pass `"SELECT 1"`; the actual query is auto-generated from `steps`.
- **`retention`** — can be omitted or pass `""`; data is fetched from the retention pipe directly.
- **All other types** — required; must be a valid SQL string.

chart_type.description:
Visualization type. Determines which other fields are required:

| `chart_type` | Extra required fields |
|---|---|
| `table` | `query` |
| `number` | `query` (must return 1 row × 1 column) |
| `bar` | `query`, `x_axis`, `y_axis` (≥ 1) |
| `line` | `query`, `x_axis`, `y_axis` (≥ 1) |
| `pie` | `query`, `y_axis` (exactly 1) |
| `stacked` | `query`, `x_axis`, `y_axis` (exactly 1), `group_by` |
| `funnel` | `steps` (≥ 2), `query` placeholder `"SELECT 1"` |
| `user_paths` | `query`, `settings.startStep` |
| `retention` | none (`query` ignored) |

title.description: Display name shown on the chart and board.

x_axis.description: Column name for the X axis. Required for `bar`, `line`, and `stacked`.

y_axis.description:
Column name(s) used as Y axis metrics.

- `bar` / `line` — at least 1 element required.
- `pie` / `stacked` — exactly 1 element required.

group_by.description: Column to group / stack series by. Required for `stacked`.

steps.description:
Ordered list of funnel steps. Required for `funnel` (minimum 2 steps).

Each element is a `FunnelStep` — add property filters as extra keys on the step object (e.g. `"rdns": { "op": "equals", "value": "io.metamask" }`).

Chart.description: A saved chart attached to a board.

query.description: SQL query powering the chart. For `funnel` and `retention` charts this is a system-managed placeholder.

x_axis.description: Column used as the X axis.

y_axis.description: Column(s) used as Y axis metric(s).

steps.description: Ordered list of funnel steps. Only present when `chart_type` is `funnel`.

settings.description: Type-specific configuration. See `ChartSettings` for all fields.

Profile.description: Comprehensive wallet profile with onchain and offchain data

EventContext.description: Contextual information about the event environment

screen_density.description: Pixel density of the device screen (devicePixelRatio)

viewport_width.description: Width of the browser viewport in pixels

viewport_height.description: Height of the browser viewport in pixels

location.description: Geographic location country code (e.g., US, NG)

EventProperties.description: Event-specific properties. Can contain any key-value pairs relevant to the event.

channel.description: Source of the event. The Formo Web SDK uses `web`; mobile SDK uses `mobile`; server SDK uses `server`. Use `api` for direct HTTP submissions and `import` for backfills.

version.description: SDK schema version. Web SDK 1.x emits `1`; legacy clients emit `0`.

tag_id.description: Label identifier (lowercased on write). e.g. vip, airdrop_eligible, coinbase.verified_account

value.description: Optional label value (e.g. tier name, country code)

chain_id.description: Optional chain identifier the label applies to

field.description: Field path. Profile: users.net_worth_usd, users.volume, users.revenue, users.points. Engagement: users.device, users.browser, users.os, users.location. Lifecycle: users.lifecycle. Socials: users.ens, users.farcaster, etc. Chains: chains.balance, chains.{chain_id}.balance. Apps: apps.{app_id}.balance. Tokens: tokens.{address}.balance. Labels: labels.{tag_id}

value.description: Filter value (string, number, boolean, or array)

scope.description: For token filters: any=wallet+protocol, protocol=specific app

RestApiError.description: Deprecated alias for `Error`. Existing endpoint specs reference this name; new specs should reference `Error` directly.

AnalyticsResponse.description: Raw Tinybird pipe response. The `data` array contains the analytics rows; the exact row shape depends on the endpoint.

IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKey.description: Optional unique value (e.g. a UUID v4) that lets you safely retry POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE requests. The first request runs normally; subsequent requests with the same key replay the stored response (status + body) for 24 hours, so retries can never double-create or double-charge. Two concurrent requests with the same key return `409 IDEMPOTENCY_IN_PROGRESS`. Generate a fresh key per logical operation.

AnalyticsDateFrom.name: date_from
AnalyticsDateFrom.description: Inclusive start date (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to 7 days before date_to.

AnalyticsDateTo.name: date_to
AnalyticsDateTo.description: Inclusive end date (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to today.

AnalyticsFilters.name: filters
AnalyticsFilters.description: Array of filter conditions, JSON-encoded in the query string. Each entry is `{ field, op, value }`. Use `in` / `notIn` with a pipe-delimited `value` (e.g. `"chrome|firefox"`) for multi-value matching.

AnalyticsOffset.name: offset
AnalyticsOffset.description: Number of results to skip for pagination (default 0)

BadRequest.description: The request was rejected — typically Zod validation failure. `code` is `INVALID_VALIDATION_REQUEST` and `details` contains a `{ fieldPath: message }` map.

details.body.name: String must contain at least 1 character(s)

Forbidden.description: The API key is valid but lacks the required scope for this endpoint.

error.message: API key missing required scope: alerts:write

NotFound.description: The requested resource does not exist or is not visible to this API key's workspace.

Conflict.description: The request conflicts with current resource state, or an `Idempotency-Key` request with the same key is currently in flight.

error.message: A request with this Idempotency-Key is already in progress. Retry shortly.

TooManyRequests.description: Per-workspace rate limit exceeded. Inspect the `RateLimit-*` response headers and back off.

InternalServerError.description: An unexpected error occurred on the server. The error has been logged; retry with exponential backoff.

x-api-scopes.description: API key scopes control access to endpoints. Create keys with the required scopes in Team Settings > API Keys.

scopes.boards:read: List and get boards and charts
scopes.boards:write: Create, update, delete boards and charts (requires boards:read)
scopes.query:read: Execute SQL analytics queries and read pre-built analytics endpoints (KPIs, top pages, lifecycle, retention, revenue, etc.)
scopes.mcp:read: MCP protocol access (analytics tools + management tools based on other scopes)

get.description: List all alerts for the project scoped to the API key.

post.description: Create a new alert for the project.

eventAlertWithWebhook.summary: Alert on swap events from mobile devices with webhook

eventAlertWithUtmFilter.summary: Alert on events from specific UTM campaigns

userAlertWithLocationFilter.summary: Alert when new users spike from a specific country

eventAlertWithNumericThreshold.summary: Alert when users hold >5 tokens in a specific app

data.title: Revenue Dashboard
data.description: Weekly revenue, conversion, and retention metrics.

example.title: Revenue Dashboard
example.description: Weekly revenue, conversion, and retention metrics.

isPublic.description: Whether the board is publicly accessible

patch.description: Update board title, description, and/or public visibility.

delete.description: Delete a board and all its charts.

data.title: Daily revenue (last 30 days)
data.query: SELECT toDate(timestamp) AS day, sum(revenue) AS revenue FROM events WHERE event = 'transaction' AND timestamp >= now() - INTERVAL 30 DAY GROUP BY day ORDER BY day

funnelWithPropertyFilters.summary: Funnel with per-step property filters (MetaMask on mainnet)

funnelOpenMultiValue.summary: Open funnel with multi-value `in` filter and breakdown

value.query: SELECT toDate(timestamp) AS date, countDistinct(address) AS users FROM events GROUP BY date ORDER BY date
value.title: Daily Active Users

value.query: SELECT toDate(timestamp) AS date, countDistinct(address) AS daily_active_users FROM events GROUP BY date ORDER BY date DESC LIMIT 30
value.title: Daily Active Users

value.query: SELECT device, COUNT(*) AS session_count FROM sessions GROUP BY device ORDER BY session_count DESC LIMIT 10
value.title: Sessions by Device

stackedChart.summary: Sessions by device grouped by browser — stacked chart

value.query: SELECT device, browser, COUNT(*) AS session_count FROM sessions GROUP BY device, browser ORDER BY session_count DESC
value.title: Sessions by Device and Browser

numberChart.summary: Total connected wallets — number / KPI card

value.query: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT address) FROM events WHERE type = 'connect'
value.title: Total Connected Wallets

value.query: SELECT * FROM events ORDER BY timestamp DESC LIMIT 10
value.title: Recent Events

userPathsChart.summary: User flow from connect — max 5 steps

value.query: SELECT anonymous_id, type AS event, timestamp FROM events WHERE timestamp >= today() - INTERVAL 30 DAY ORDER BY anonymous_id, timestamp
value.title: Post-Connect User Flow

userPathsOpenEnded.summary: Open-ended user flow from page view

value.query: SELECT anonymous_id, type AS event, timestamp FROM events WHERE timestamp >= today() - INTERVAL 14 DAY ORDER BY anonymous_id, timestamp
value.title: User Discovery Paths

retentionFiltered.summary: Weekly retention — desktop users, transaction event

example.title: Daily revenue (last 30 days)
example.query: SELECT toDate(timestamp) AS day, sum(revenue) AS revenue FROM events WHERE event = 'transaction' AND timestamp >= now() - INTERVAL 30 DAY GROUP BY day ORDER BY day

post.description: Add a blockchain contract to monitor.

highValueMobileUsers.summary: High-value mobile users from paid campaigns

excludeUSUsers.summary: All users except from the US

powerUsers.summary: Power users with high net worth from organic traffic

multiChainWhaleUsers.summary: Users active on 3+ chains with high net worth

behavioralFilterSimple.summary: Users who performed 'signature' event at least once in last 30 days
behavioralFilterSimple.description: Behavioural filters use the 'events' filter key with a base64-encoded JSON array as the value. The JSON contains event name, frequency operator (greater/greaterOrEqual/equals/lessOrEqual/less), times count, and date range.

behavioralFilterWithProperties.summary: Users who connected via MetaMask on Ethereum mainnet + from India
behavioralFilterWithProperties.description: Combines a behavioural filter (type=connect event with property filters) and a demographic filter (location). The 'event' field uses the event type key (e.g. 'connect', 'signature', 'transaction', 'page'), not the display label. Event property filters use {op, value} objects as additional keys.

behavioralFilterMultipleEvents.summary: Users with 5+ page views last week AND at least 1 transaction last month
behavioralFilterMultipleEvents.description: Multiple behavioural events in a single filter. All conditions in the array must be met (AND logic). Valid event types: page, screen, connect, disconnect, chain, signature, transaction, track, decoded_log, detect, identify.

total.description: Total number of profiles across all pages.

page.description: 1-indexed page number echoed from the request.

size.description: Page size echoed from the request.

has_more.description: True if there is at least one more page after this one.

chainFilter.summary: Users active on Ethereum with >$1k balance

tokenFilter.summary: Users holding USDC in any protocol

put.description: Set first-party properties for a wallet profile. Override display name, email, socials, avatar, location, and other identity fields.

schema.description: Merge-update of profile properties. Only the listed keys are accepted; unknown keys are rejected. At least one key must be provided.

400.description: Invalid request (bad address, empty body, or no allowed keys)

403.description: API key lacks the required scope

post.description: Upsert one or more labels for a wallet. Accepts either a single label object or an array of labels. Labels with the same tag_id (and chain_id, if provided) are overwritten. Requires profiles:write scope.

400.description: Invalid request (bad address, missing tag_id, etc.)

403.description: API key lacks the required scope

delete.description: Delete a label from a wallet. Pass chain_id to scope the deletion to a specific chain; omit it to match labels without a chain scope. Requires profiles:write scope.

chain_id.description: Optional chain identifier to scope the deletion

400.description: Invalid request (bad address or missing tag_id)

403.description: API key lacks the required scope

post.description: Import wallet addresses into the project. Requires profiles:write scope and Scale/Enterprise plan.

Execute a SQL query against the project's analytics data. Only SELECT and WITH statements are allowed. LIMIT is capped at 1,000,000. Forbidden keywords: INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, DROP, ALTER, TRUNCATE, CREATE, etc.

dailyActiveUsers.summary: Daily active users over last 30 days

value.query: SELECT toDate(timestamp) as date, uniq(anonymous_id) as dau FROM events WHERE timestamp >= now() - interval 30 day GROUP BY date ORDER BY date
SELECT event, count() as total FROM events WHERE timestamp >= now() - interval 7 day GROUP BY event ORDER BY total DESC LIMIT 10

200.description: Offset-paginated query results. The server doesn't own pagination here — `LIMIT` and `OFFSET` come from your SQL string and are echoed back. `total` is the row count before LIMIT was applied (Tinybird's `rows_before_limit_at_least`); `has_more` is true when there are additional rows beyond the current window.

total.description: Total rows before LIMIT was applied.

limit.description: Applied LIMIT (parsed from your SQL; defaults to the server cap if absent).

offset.description: Applied OFFSET (parsed from your SQL; 0 if absent).

has_more.description: True when `offset + data.length < total` — i.e. there's another page to fetch by re-running with a higher OFFSET.

get.summary: Get KPIs (visitors, pageviews, bounce rate, session duration)
get.description: Time-series traffic KPIs with optional dimension breakdown. Returns visitors, pageviews, bounce rate and average session length.

parameters.name: include_previous_period
parameters.description: Return current and previous period for WoW comparison

get.summary: Get user lifecycle stages (New / Returning / Power / Resurrected / Churned)
get.description: Counts wallet users by lifecycle stage based on activity within the date range. The reference date is `date_to`.

parameters.name: include_previous_period
parameters.description: Return current and previous period for WoW comparison

get.summary: Get revenue and transaction volume time-series

parameters.name: include_previous_period
parameters.description: Return current and previous period for WoW comparison

meta.name: metric
meta.type: Tuple(project_id String, user_count UInt64, day7_retained UInt64, day30_retained UInt64, day90_retained UInt64, day7_retention_rate Float64, day30_retention_rate Float64, day90_retention_rate Float64)

meta.name: user
meta.type: Array(Tuple(project_id String, cohort_week Date, num_users UInt64, week_0 Float64, week_1 Float64, week_2 Float64, week_3 Float64, week_4 Float64, week_5 Float64, week_6 Float64, week_7 Float64, week_8 Float64, week_9 Float64, week_10 Float64, week_11 Float64, week_12 Float64))

get.summary: Get top pages by visits or users

parameters.name: metric_column
parameters.description: Column to break down sources by. Use `channel` for the 12-channel acquisition classifier (see docs/channels.md).

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Pass 'custom' to filter to custom track events only (events with type='track' and a non-empty event name). Omit for all events.

get.description: Most frequent events in the project. By default returns all event categories (page / track / identify / decoded_log / etc.). Pass `type=custom` to filter to custom track events only.

get.summary: Get revenue grouped by a chosen column

parameters.name: metric_column
parameters.description: Column to group revenue by. Use `channel` for the 12-channel acquisition classifier (see docs/channels.md). Unknown values return zero rows.

get.summary: Get transaction volume grouped by a chosen column

parameters.name: metric_column
parameters.description: Column to group volume by. Use `channel` for the 12-channel acquisition classifier (see docs/channels.md). Unknown values return zero rows.

post.description: Send analytics events to Formo. This endpoint runs on events.formo.so (not api.formo.so). Authenticate with your project's SDK write key.

tags.name: Query
tags.description: Execute SQL queries and call pre-built analytics endpoints (KPIs, top pages, lifecycle, retention, revenue). Requires the query:read scope.
